company_name: WISE - World Innovation Summit for Education

property_scores.justification: Precise, well-evidenced value (Education) with multiple corroborating sources (mission, portfolio, Qatar Foundation alignment).
Highly specific niche articulation with concrete evidence of vertical specialization (EdTech, AI-in-education, girls' education, policy).
Clear platform/infrastructure positioning with strong evidence of convening, funding, and knowledge-production role.
Growth assessment is reasonable but confidence is medium — distinguishes global EdTech market growth from WISE's niche maturity, though evidence is somewhat indirect.
Unambiguous grant/endowment model with high certainty — WISE has no commercial revenue, funded entirely by Qatar Foundation.
Precise hybrid delivery (events + grants + research) with clear evidence of each component and their respective mechanisms.
Fixed-cost-heavy assessment is well-reasoned but lacks precise budget breakdown; confidence is medium because actual cost ratios are not disclosed.
Exceptionally detailed multi-layered moat analysis with concrete evidence for each layer (sovereign backing, patronage, brand equity, network density, flywheel).
Comprehensive articulation of five critical dependencies with specific evidence for each (funding, patronage, network quality, logistics, research partnerships).
Estimated 11-50 range with ~36 FTE is reasonable but not definitive; evidence is from secondary sources (Crunchbase, RocketReach) rather than primary disclosure.
Marked as not applicable with speculative $5-15M estimate; lacks confidence because WISE is non-revenue entity with no public budget disclosure.
Clear sovereign/endowment-funded model with strong evidence of Qatar Foundation backing and no external fundraising or runway pressure.
Asset-light assessment is sound with reasonable evidence, but confidence is medium because precise asset inventory is not disclosed publicly.
Structurally sustainable position is well-supported; sovereign funding removes commercial sustainability risk, making financial health assessment clear.
Established (founded 2009, 17 years old) with high certainty from founding date and continuous biennial summits since inception.
Well-articulated with concrete scale metrics (35K community, 150K followers, 4K summit attendees, 12K cumulative across summits, 4.9K prize applications).
Clear mature/optimization phase with evidence of strategic reinvention under new CEO; distinguishes stable format from deliberate pivot toward implementation and Africa focus.
Three distinct, well-articulated segments (policymakers, innovators, philanthropy) with concrete evidence for each from speaker profiles, prize recipients, and consortium partners.
Niche specialist positioning is clear with competitive analysis, though peer comparison is somewhat limited; high confidence but not absolute precision on market share claims.
Community/mission-driven GTM is clear and distinct; strong evidence of reputation-led engagement, no sales team, and invitation-based participation model.
Tension between single-funder and diversified stakeholders is well-articulated; high confidence in Qatar Foundation 100% funding dependency and stakeholder diversity.
Not applicable assessment is reasonable given no commercial pricing; coverage is high (no ambiguity) but the property is not applicable to this business model.
Professional CEO-led governance is clear with specific individuals identified (Yiannouka tenure, Kazmi appointment); high confidence in leadership model.
Flat functional structure inference is reasonable from available evidence (named functional roles, 36 FTE size), but precise org chart is not publicly disclosed.
Co-located Doha assessment is sound with evidence of HQ and summit location, but remote/hybrid composition is not explicitly confirmed; medium confidence.
Domain-expert-heavy profile is well-evidenced through leadership backgrounds (Kazmi doctorate + 30 years policy, Yiannouka McKinsey + LKY), reflecting policy/research talent composition.
Minimal tech assessment is clear and justified; high coverage as non-tech organisation with standard tools, medium confidence due to limited public evidence of internal systems.
Not applicable is appropriate and clearly justified; WISE has no software engineering function, so engineering practices are not relevant.
Basic analytics assessment is reasonable but largely speculative; low confidence due to lack of public visibility into actual data infrastructure maturity.
Basic-to-intermediate assessment is generic and not well-evidenced; low confidence as no specific security posture details are available publicly.
Low-to-moderate regulatory burden assessment is reasonable but somewhat generic; medium confidence due to limited visibility into actual compliance requirements.
Global multi-continent scope is well-articulated with specific evidence of consortium members, prize recipients, and attendee geographies.
Recession-resistant classification is clearly justified through sovereign endowment funding and insulation from commercial cycles.
State-backed governance structure is clear with evidence of QF foundation governance and alignment with Qatar national education strategy.
Pragmatic-to-fast-follower posture is well-evidenced through WISE 12 theme and prize selections; high confidence with slight caution on inferring internal culture.
Thought-leader/visionary positioning is clearly articulated with evidence from summit framing, prize narrative, and media coverage positioning.
Mission-driven values are explicitly evidenced through prize criteria, speaker selection, CEO background, and organisational programming.
Strong-in-niche employer assessment is reasonable from leadership credentials and Qatar Foundation prestige, but limited visibility into broader employer perception.

notes: WISE data quality is strong across mission-aligned dimensions (industry, business model, market position, operating environment, culture/brand). Weaker areas are technical maturity (minimal tech organisation) and internal operational details (org structure, internal talent visibility). Financial metrics are constrained by grant-funding model with no independent revenue disclosure. Single high-confidence finding: WISE is a mature, institutionally-moored education-innovation convener with sovereign backing and multi-layered competitive moats.
